channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Demystifying On-Demand Capacity Reservations
item.description: About On-Demand Capacity Reservations Introducing the “parking garage” metaphor There are dozens of VM types available in Azure which span multiple generations of CPU across vendors and architectures. Within each Azure region are datacenters hosting pools of hardware which runs Azure services, such...
item.dc:creator: KenHooverMSFT

item.title: DevSecOps on AKS: Governance Gates That Actually Prevent Incidents
item.description: This article is for AKS platform/infra engineers, SREs, and security teams who want a practical, enforceable model for stopping common Kubernetes misconfigurations before they become incidents—without turning delivery into bureaucracy. Why incidents still happen after “adding security to the...
item.dc:creator: lakshaymalik

item.title: How can I repair a mov file as corrupted on my windows laptop?
item.description: I have an important MOV video file that suddenly became corrupted. It won't play properly. I'm getting errors, black screen, or the video just freezes. I'm guessing it might be from an interrupted recording, improper file transfer, or maybe a camera crash.I'm looking for advice on how to repair a...
item.dc:creator: HoltSawye

item.title: Azure SQL Managed Instance as an AI‑Enabled PaaS Platform
item.description: AI Capabilities Built into Azure SQL Managed Instance Azure SQL MI includes multiple intelligence layers by default: Intelligent Insights for anomaly detection Automatic tuning (recommend mode) Copilot‑assisted diagnostics Native vector data types for AI workloads These capabilities work together...
item.dc:creator: ShivaniThadiyan

item.title: Look for a QR code creator free and no expiration
item.description: Hi,I need to create some QR codes for labels that will be used long-term (product packaging and signage). I tested a few free qr code creation websites, but many of them either add an expiration date (e.g., 7 days or 30 days for dynamic QR codes) or require a paid plan to keep them active beyond a...
item.dc:creator: Gordony

item.title: Viva Engage Communities Gain Support for Sensitivity Labels
item.description: Viva Engage communities now support Purview container management labels (sensitivity labels that manage settings for groups, teams, communities, etc.). The update makes it possible to manage community settings like privacy through labels instead of settings applied to individual communities. Once...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: AI‑Assisted Azure Infrastructure Validation and Drift Detection
item.description: Why Traditional Drift Detection Isn’t Enough Most teams already rely on: Terraform plan reviews Azure Policy compliance dashboards Azure Resource Graph queries Manual scripts and audits The problem isn’t missing data—it’s interpretation at scale. Validation outputs are: Verbose and noisy Spread...
item.dc:creator: ShivaniThadiyan

item.title: PC always boots to BIOS instead of Windows Computer
item.description: I’ve had this issue for a while now, ever since my first boot. Whenever I turn on my computer I’m sent to the BIOS menu and have to boot override to windows boot manager to get to Windows. My boot option #1 is windows boot manager. Does anyone have any tips for how to fix this?
item.dc:creator: Brycennin

item.title: Can no longer open "extendtext.exe" after Windows update yesterday.
item.description: I've never had this issue. I've been using RMXP for about a year now with no issues and then suddenly this morning, I can no longer run "extendtext.exe" without getting this error. I had a Windows update yesterday and I'm guessing that's the cause of the issue since I've found 1 other person who is...
item.dc:creator: Lawrencepp

item.title: MIL who lives in another state sent me this screen cap several hours ago - how bad is it?
item.description: Says she clicked on a Gmail shortcut, which I created on her desktop months ago only because she's prone to typing in or clicking on the wrong URL, she says that's all she did then BAM the mouse locked up, screen littered with over 20 popups, and a "Your computer is infected, call Microsoft Tech...
item.dc:creator: Leroy95

item.title: 🚀 General Availability of Private Application Gateway on Azure Application Gateway v2
item.description: 🔍 What Is Private Application Gateway? Historically, Application Gateway v2 required a public IP address to communicate with the Azure control plane (GatewayManager). This requirement imposed several constraints: Mandatory public IP exposure Restricted Network Security Group (NSG) rules Limited...
item.dc:creator: kumaramit1

item.title: Stylish and cute font generator that works on my windows pc?
item.description: Hi all,I want to create those aesthetic or cute text styles (like fancy Instagram bios or usernames), but I'm on a Windows PC and not sure which tools are worth using. A lot of sites pop up in search, but some look sketchy or don't copy properly.Ideally looking for something simple: type text → get...
item.dc:creator: DelawareCanal

item.title: oBeaver — A Beaver That Runs LLMs on Your Machine 🦫
item.description: Hi there! I'm the creator of oBeaver. This project started from a pretty simple desire: I wanted to run large language models on my own computer. No data sent to the cloud. No API keys. No per-call charges. I'm guessing you've had the same thought. There are already great tools out there — Ollama...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: Enterprise‑Scale Azure Subscription Vending Using Azure Verified Modules (AVM)
item.description: Why Subscription Vending Is Critical at Scale Azure subscriptions define the security, governance, and billing boundary for workloads. In large organizations, manual subscription creation often leads to: Inconsistent management group placement Delayed or missing policy enforcement Incorrect RBAC...
item.dc:creator: kumaramit1

item.title: How to delete apps on mac that won't delete?
item.description: Okay, this is driving me a bit crazy!Trying to clean up my Mac, but a few apps just won't delete. I drag them to Trash, and either nothing happens or they come back after restart. Some even say they’re in use when I'm pretty sure they're not. What is the proper way to delete apps on mac that won't...
item.dc:creator: CaliforniaGolden

item.title: Blue‑Green Strategy for Always‑On TCP Workloads on Azure Container Apps
item.description: Scenario: Always‑on workloads in Azure Container Apps continuously pull from a TCP source, process the stream, and push into Azure Managed Redis, which is then consumed by another always‑on Container Apps workload that writes to a database. Challenge: Standard revision traffic splitting isn’t a fit...
item.dc:creator: srivastavani

item.title: Bring Your Own Model (BYOM) for Azure AI Applications using Azure Machine Learning
item.description: Modern AI-powered applications running on Azure increasingly require flexibility in model choice. While managed model catalogs accelerate time to value, real-world enterprise applications often need to: Host open‑source or fine‑tuned models Deploy domain‑specific or regulated models inside a tenant...
item.dc:creator: vaibhavpandey

item.title: Windows spotlight image missing in the lock screen Dev.
item.description: hi all,My Windows spotlight image is missing in the lock screen. In the feedback hub, I think it is reported but want to double check if it is actually a bug. Anyone facing this issue?
item.dc:creator: paulpchou

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quick Start with TypeScript
item.description: Azure Functions makes hosting MCP apps simple: build locally, create a secure endpoint, and deploy fast with azd. What Are MCP Apps? MCP (Model Context Protocol) apps add new features to AI assistants and clients: MCP Tools: Remote functions that clients call to perform actions (for example, fetch...
item.dc:creator: swapnil_nagar

item.title: AKS cluster with AGIC hits the Azure Application Gateway backend pool limit (100)
item.description: I’m writing this article to document a real-world scaling issue we hit while exposing many applications from an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster using Application Gateway Ingress Controller (AGIC). The problem is easy to miss because Kubernetes resources keep applying successfully, but the...
item.dc:creator: kkaushal

item.title: An update to the active flow billing model for Azure SRE Agent
item.description: Earlier today, we announced that Azure SRE Agent now supports multiple AI model providers, starting with Anthropic. To support multi-model choice, and make active usage costs easier to understand, we’re updating how active flow usage is measured, effective April 15, 2026. At a glance What’s...
item.dc:creator: Mayunk_Jain

item.title: Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions
item.description: This blog post is for developers who have an MCP server deployed to Azure Functions and want to connect it to Microsoft Foundry agents. It walks through why you'd want to do this, the different authentication options available, and how to get your agent calling your MCP tools. Connect your MCP...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: Azure SRE Agent now supports multiple model providers, including Anthropic Claude
item.description: Today, Azure SRE Agent adds model provider selection—choose between Azure OpenAI and Anthropic to match the right AI provider to your incident workflow. SRE Agent has saved over 20,000 engineering hours by pulling together logs, deployments, and signals into a single investigation thread, turning...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Windows 12 Is Not a Rumor. It’s Already in Your System
item.description: The new build 29558 reveals way more than most people think. If you know where to look, you’ll see that the next Windows release is already very close.Side‑by‑Side Winners now includes new versions of core system modulesStartup‑task‑data is updated across all architecturesDefender and Networking UI...
item.dc:creator: kikero_exe

item.title: Accelerate Agent Development: Hacks for Building with Microsoft Sentinel data lake
item.description: As a Senior Product Manager | Developer Architect on the App Assure team working to bring Microsoft Sentinel and Security Copilot solutions to market, I interact with many ISVs building agents on Microsoft Sentinel data lake for the first time. I’ve written this article to walk you through one...
item.dc:creator: MitchellGulledge

item.title: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description: The GitHub Copilot SDK is now available in public preview. This gives you the building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities directly into your own applications, workflows, and platform services.… The post Copilot SDK in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Kubernetes gated deployment is now generally available for AKS automatic clusters. Use help to deploy the Defender for Containers sensor to use this feature. More information can be found here. Grouped recommendations are converted into individual ones to list each...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Microsoft Entra expands SCIM support with new SCIM 2.0 APIs for identity lifecycle operations
item.description: Modern organizations rely on a growing ecosystem of applications, platforms, and services to run their business. Managing users and groups consistently across these systems is essential for security and operational efficiency. Many teams rely on the System for Cross-domain Identity Management...
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dadzie

item.title: Intune iOS User-Based App Targeting
item.description: I’ve noticed an issue with user-based targeting and was wondering if this is an issue, or I'm just using it wrong.Lets say I want an iOS app to be deployed out to a user group, but only to company owned devices of those users. I set the assignment for required user group and assign an Include...
item.dc:creator: Braaaaaad

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description: Following our enterprise-level, user-level, and organization-level CLI metrics releases, we’re completing coverage with per-user CLI breakdowns in organization reports. Organization admins can now see which individual users are active on… The post Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description: This month, GitHub Actions adds entrypoint and command overrides for service containers and new security features including OIDC custom properties and VNET failover. Customizing entrypoints for service containers Many GitHub… The post GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description: C# 15 introduces union types — declare a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. Try unions in preview today and see the broader exhaustiveness roadmap. The post Explore union types in C# 15 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Bill Wagner

item.title: Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain)
item.description: Aspire 13.2 ships aspire docs, a CLI for browsing, searching, and reading official aspire.dev docs from your terminal, with the same source of truth available to AI skills and automation. The post Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain) appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: Finding Forwards based upon to address
item.description: I have a distribution list that is designed to send an email to every mailbox associated with my company's domain. This distribution list only contains company generated mailboxes. When I send an email to this list, I get automatic replies from 2 email addresses that are not associated with my...
item.dc:creator: Michael Beaver

item.title: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description: Generative AI is upgrading cyberattacks, from 450% higher phishing click‑through rates to industrialized MFA bypass. The post Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sherrod DeGrippo

item.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description: Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description: March 2026 brought a major step forward for GitHub Copilot extensibility in Visual Studio, with custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that make the agent smarter and more capable.… The post GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description: The top-level Security tab across repositories, organizations, and enterprises has been renamed to Security & quality on github.com. This change restructures the navigation to colocate code quality findings alongside security… The post The Security tab is now Security & quality appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description: Finding the right issue just got easier. First introduced in public preview in January and expanded to the Issues dashboard in February, improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally… The post Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description: Organization custom instructions for GitHub Copilot, first introduced in April 2025, are now generally available. With organization custom instructions, Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organization administrators can set default instructions… The post Copilot organization custom...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Windows 11 make new windows open centered
item.description: Windows 11 remembers last window position. I want all new windows to open centered vertically. Tried registry & FancyZones, no luck. Any simple fix?
item.dc:creator: HoltSawye

item.title: What is Secured-core PC on Windows 11?
item.description: I want a brief explanation of the Secured-core PC feature in Windows Security. What does it do and what security does it provide?
item.dc:creator: YatesGriffin

item.title: How to revert Windows 11 search bar font to old style?
item.description: Microsoft updated the search bar font in Windows 11, and I dislike the new one. How can I change it back to the old font?
item.dc:creator: BreckenFoster

item.title: Restricting App Creation of SharePoint Online Sites
item.description: This article discusses the use of restricted site creation for third-party Entra ID apps. The feature has an allow or deny list to identify apps that can create new SharePoint Online sites. Controlling the set of apps that can create new sites contributes to limiting site sprawl and makes sure that...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Five reasons to attend SQLBits in the UK
item.description: Microsoft SQL team is returning as headline sponsor of SQLBits 2026, taking place from 22–25 April at ICC Wales in Newport. I’m speaking for the whole team when I say we’d love to see you there. I’m here today to give you five reasons you should attend SQLBits. This year we’re teaming up with AMD …...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Unlocking AI-Ready Unstructured Data at Scale with Komprise and Azure
item.description: Why Move Unstructured Data to Azure On-premises storage environments are often over-provisioned to accommodate future growth, driving costs and operational complexity. Azure’s cloud-based storage platform enables organizations to right-size their environments through elastic scaling and Microsoft’s...
item.dc:creator: dmalbrough

item.title: VS Code Custom Agents: AI-Powered Terraform Security Scanning in the IDE
item.description: GitHub Copilot is already a powerful coding assistant, but out of the box it knows nothing specific about your project's conventions, security requirements, or operational processes. Custom agents change that. They let you define specialized AI assistants that live inside your repository, carry...
item.dc:creator: SundarBalajiA

item.title: How to completely remove or uninstall norton antivirus security on windows 11
item.description: I already uninstalled Norton Antivirus using the standard Windows "Add or Remove Programs" option, but I keep seeing leftover files, background services, and even registry entries related to Norton. It's also blocking certain network settings and causing conflicts with my new antivirus.I want to...
item.dc:creator: WaylonDiesel

item.title: Dataflows: Thank you for eight years of Gen1—and why Gen2 is the future
item.description: For more than eight years, customers have relied on Power BI Dataflows (Gen1) as a core part of their analytics solutions. We’re grateful for the trust you placed in the Power Query experience to build reusable, low-code data preparation pipelines that power reports, semantic models, and downstream...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Build a Fully Offline AI App with Foundry Local and CAG
item.description: A hands-on guide to building an on-device AI support agent using Context-Augmented Generation, JavaScript, and Foundry Local. You have probably heard the AI pitch: "just call our API." But what happens when your application needs to work without an internet connection? Perhaps your users are field...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott
